idea-plugin.name: MCP Control
idea-plugin.change-notes:
0.5.0
  • Breakpoint mutations (add, update, remove) now return the full breakpoint list with (new)/(updated) highlights
  • Batch operations: breakpoint_add accepts comma-separated locations, breakpoint_update accepts comma-separated IDs
  • Method breakpoint detection: adding a breakpoint on a function definition line is blocked with guidance to add manually
  • Stack trace collapsing: 3+ consecutive library frames collapsed by default, expand_stack parameter to override
  • Session-breakpoint cross-reference: session_list shows which breakpoint a paused session is at
  • Smarter session switch detection: terminated sessions auto-track the next session instead of blocking
  • Evaluate tool description clarifies frame #0 limitation (xdebug constraint)
  • Fix: circular reference false positives on arrays with size info (e.g. array[7])
  • Cleaner output: "Current breakpoints:" header on remove/partial-failure responses
  • Leaner evaluate output: removed redundant source position header
0.4.0
  • New tool: breakpoint_add_exception — break on any PHP exception class by name
  • Short name lookup with disambiguation (RuntimeException → lists all matches if ambiguous)
  • Only classes extending \Exception or \Error are accepted
  • Exception breakpoints work with existing breakpoint_list, breakpoint_update, and breakpoint_remove
0.3.2
  • Rebranding: MCP Hub → MCP Control
0.3.1
  • Fix: .mcp.json installer now includes required "type": "http" field
0.3.0
  • New tool: debug_console — read stdout/stderr from the debug session's console (useful for Docker/remote PHP processes)
  • Fix: stopping a session now auto-activates the next alive session instead of leaving a dead tab focused
  • Smarter session errors: when the active session ends but other sessions exist, the error lists them with guidance to use session_activate
  • Cleaner session switch alert messages
0.2.1
  • Show plugin version in tool window status line
0.2.0
  • New tool: session_activate — switch active debug session (like clicking a debug tab)
  • Removed session_id parameter from all debug tools — simpler API, fewer tokens
  • Session switch detection: agent gets an alert if the user or IDE switches the active session unexpectedly
  • MCP server version now matches the plugin version
0.1.1
  • Fix: Settings page now visible under Settings > Tools > MCP Control
0.1.0
  • Initial release
  • 13 MCP tools: breakpoints, sessions, navigation, inspection
  • Tool window with activity log, start/stop, settings
  • One-click .mcp.json installation
  • Configurable port and auto-start per project
idea-plugin.description:
MCP server that gives AI agents access to IDE features they can't get from the command line.

Agents can read files and run terminals on their own. They can't set breakpoints, step through code, or inspect variables. MCP Control exposes these IDE-only capabilities as MCP tools.

15 tools across 4 categories

Breakpoints (work without an active debug session)
  • list, add, update, remove — with batch support (comma-separated)
  • Exception breakpoints — pause on any PHP exception class
  • Conditions, log expressions, suspend control
  • Method breakpoint detection — prevents wrong breakpoint type
Session management
  • List active debug sessions, stop one or all
  • Switch active session (all debug tools operate on the active session)
Navigation (requires paused session)
  • Step over / into / out / continue
  • Run to a specific line
  • Every step returns a snapshot: source context, variables, call stack (library frames auto-collapsed)
Inspection (requires paused session)
  • Full debug snapshot (source + variables + stack trace)
  • Expand variables to any depth (circular references detected)
  • Switch stack frames and inspect variables at that scope
  • Evaluate PHP expressions with side effects ($bar = 'new value')
  • Read console output (stdout/stderr) — works with Docker/remote processes

Setup

  1. Open the MCP Control tool window (bottom panel)
  2. Click "Install .mcp.json" to configure your MCP client
  3. Start the server

Works with Claude Code, Cursor, Claude Desktop, and any other MCP client. Configurable port, optional auto-start per project.

How debugging works

You start debug sessions in PhpStorm as usual. The agent interacts with them through MCP tools: setting breakpoints, stepping through code, inspecting state. You both see the same debug session. Pair debugging with an AI.
